fyPerformance: Q4 consolidated revenue grew 8%, supported by 7% underlying sales growth.
Growth was primarily volume-led and marked the highest quarterly growth in 12 quarters.
EBITDA grew 6% YoY with margin at 23.7%, at the higher end of guidance.
Reported PAT grew 20% YoY, while PAT before exceptional items grew 4%.
Cash flow from operations stood close to 10,500 Cr for FY26.
highlights: Beauty & Wellbeing grew 8% USG.
Personal Care delivered 5% underlying sales growth.
Foods business crossed 4,000 Cr revenue.
Quick commerce channel grew over 25%.
Minimalist annual revenue run rate is around 850 Cr.
Management highlighted improving rural and volume trends.
Hindustan Unilever Ltd.quote: "This represents our highest quarterly growth in 12 quarters."

fyPerformance: Consolidated sales volume grew 16.3%, led by India growth of 14.4%.
International territories grew 21.4% in volume during the quarter.
Revenue grew 18.1% YoY to 6,574 Cr.
EBITDA grew 21% with margin improving 55 bps to 23.3%.
PAT grew 20.1% YoY, supported by volume growth and operating efficiencies.
highlights: India EBITDA margin improved by 112 bps.
Gross margin improved 62 bps to 55.2%.
Dairy segment grew around 60%.
International business contributed around 63% of volumes.
Twizza revenue was around 800 Cr and Crickley around 160 Cr.
Interim dividend of 0.50 per share was declared.
Varun Beverages Ltd.quote: "We have enough capacity that even if we get a 50% growth, we can comfortably handle it."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue crossed the 2,000 Cr mark, growing 9.1% YoY.
Q4 revenue grew 8.9% YoY to 485.9 Cr.
Biscuits grew 8.4% YoY in Q4, while Bakery grew 8.5%.
FY26 EBITDA stood at 257.7 Cr with margin at 12.6%.
PAT stood at 140.9 Cr with PAT margin at 6.9%.
highlights: Bakery delivered 14% growth for FY26.
Q4 EBITDA margin improved 25 bps YoY to 12.7%.
Mumbai plant was commissioned in Q4FY26.
Biscuits and Bakery have compounded strongly over the last four years.
B2B opportunity improves as new capacity ramps up.
Management remains watchful on input cost pressure.
Mrs. Bectors Food Specialities Ltd.quote: "FY26 marks a significant milestone as we crossed the 2,000 Cr revenue mark."

fyPerformance: More than 95% of the portfolio gained or sustained market share.
Foods exited FY26 at over 1,000 Cr revenue.
Premium Personal Care digital-first portfolio exited FY26 at 1,100 Cr.
VAHO saw over 20% volume growth in Q4.
Bangladesh sales growth accelerated sharply to 35% in Q4.
highlights: Foods portfolio is now a meaningful growth engine.
Digital brands are expected to exit FY27 at double-digit EBITDA margin.
Beardo and Plix remain accelerated growth brands.
Management expects high-teen EBITDA growth in FY27.
Gross margin expansion of 350-400 bps was discussed.
Operating margin expansion of around 150 bps is targeted.
Marico Ltd.quote: "We expect to sustain high single-digit volume growth in the India business in FY27."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood near 2,994 Cr with 9.5% volume growth.
Q4 volume growth was above 16%, the highest quarterly growth during the year.
Q4 revenue crossed around 720 Cr with EBITDA of 88 Cr.
FY26 EBITDA margin improved 120 bps to 13.7%.
PAT margin in Q4 was close to 8% with PAT around 56 Cr.
highlights: Family packs grew 20%, while impulse packs grew 14%.
Core Namkeen and snacks recovered after GST reduction.
Packaged sweets performed well in Q4.
One brand crossed 100 Cr revenue during the year.
Sweet factory capex in Bikaner remains a key project.
Management expects premium and new markets to support growth.
Bikaji Foods International Ltd.quote: "Quarter 4 volume growth was upward of 16%, highest in any quarter."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 7.5% to 18,858 Cr.
FY26 PAT grew 16.3% to 2,533 Cr, with PAT margin at 13.4%.
Q4 revenue grew 7.1% to 4,686 Cr.
Q4 operating profit grew 6% to 768 Cr.
Q4 PAT grew 21.1% YoY to 678 Cr.
highlights: Operating profit for FY26 grew 11.6% to 3,208 Cr.
PBT grew 12.4% to 3,289 Cr.
Non-biscuit and new categories gained share in the revenue mix.
E-commerce grew at around 1.4x of biscuits.
Some businesses outpaced company growth by around 3x.
Management highlighted commodity moderation in parts of the portfolio.
Britannia Industries Ltd.quote: "For the year, at the revenue level, we clocked 18,858 Cr."

fyPerformance: Q4 revenue declined 4% YoY to 925 Cr in a challenging operating environment.
Core domestic business grew 11% in Q4, reflecting underlying brand strength.
FY26 revenue stood at 3,780 Cr, down 1% YoY.
Gross margin expanded 130 bps for FY26 to 69.9%.
FY26 EBITDA declined 6% to 964 Cr, while PAT stood at 775 Cr.
highlights: Pain Management grew 11% in Q4.
Kesh King grew 14%, its second consecutive double-digit quarter.
Strategic investments grew 34%.
7 Oils in One grew 34%.
Modern trade grew 70% and GT Marts grew 25%.
Talc dependency reduced to 27% of domestic sales.
Emami Ltd.quote: "We are well positioned to deliver sustained growth in FY27."

fyPerformance: Q4 consolidated revenue grew 11% on 6% underlying volume growth.
Consolidated EBITDA grew 10% with operating margin at 21.7%.
Standalone business delivered 8% UVG and 10% sales growth.
Standalone EBITDA grew 18% with margin at 24.7%.
Indonesia delivered 4% volume growth and showed signs of stabilization.

guidance.value: Growth expected to compound in FY27
Near-term oil inflation may pressure margins

highlights: Home Care grew 12% in value.
Personal Care grew 3%.
Perfumes and deodorants delivered strong double-digit growth.
Standalone margins were supported by cost control and portfolio mix.
Africa reported strong sales growth off a weak base.
Management remained confident on India volume recovery.
Godrej Consumer Products Ltd.quote: "Q4 FY26 has been a quarter of strong broad-based performance."

fyPerformance: Q4 consolidated revenue grew 18%, with India business revenue up 15%.
India business UVG was 13%, with tea volume up 4%.
Growth businesses crossed 4,000 Cr and grew 24% for the year.
Consolidated EBITDA grew 27% in Q4, ahead of revenue growth.
FY26 topline grew 15%, EBITDA grew 12%, PBT grew 23% and group net profit grew 24%.
highlights: Sampann grew 69% in Q4 and 46% for FY26.
RTD grew 28% in volume and 23% in revenue in Q4.
International business delivered 11% constant currency growth in Q4.
Non-branded business grew 41% in Q4.
Salt market share improved by 100 bps.
The company launched 80 new products during the year.

fyPerformance: FY26 standalone revenue grew 76.1% to 1,357 Cr.
FY26 EBITDA stood at 367.7 Cr with margin at 27.1%.
FY26 PAT stood at 233.2 Cr with PAT margin of 17.2%.
Q4 revenue stood at 382.3 Cr, EBITDA at 102.9 Cr and PAT at 59.5 Cr.
Operating cash flow stood at 259.4 Cr, with ROCE at 33.6% and ROE at 40.3%.
highlights: Solvent fractionation plant capacity increased 30%.
Net debt/equity stood at 0.38x.
Burkina Faso project capex is around 120 Cr.
Forward integration project forms a major part of capex.
Management indicated 25-30% growth as a reasonable broad range.
Capacity upgrades support the next growth phase.
Manorama Industries Ltd.quote: "The outlook for FY27 remains strongly positive on the topline front."

fyPerformance: FY26 group revenue grew 43% YoY.
FY26 EBITDA grew 32% to 741.4 Cr.
FY26 net profit grew 25% to 388.1 Cr.
Q4 revenue grew 46% YoY, while EBITDA grew 16%.
Domestic gross turnover was around 650 Cr, including brand sales of around 440 Cr.

guidance.value: No major capex for next 2 years

highlights: Q4 net profit grew 12% to 114.5 Cr.
Net debt reduced sharply to around 1,073 Cr.
Capacities are sufficient for growth aspirations over the next two years.
Online channel contributes around 20-25% of branded sales.
Value growth may moderate if coffee prices normalize.
Maintenance capex is expected around 25 Cr.

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 79.3% to 553.1 Cr.

FY26 PAT grew 79.8% to 72.2 Cr with PAT margin at 13.1%.

Q4 EBITDA stood at 30.6 Cr with margin at 18.5%.
highlights: Brownfield expansion increased capacity from 6,500 MT to 11,000 MT.
